intro: Oxytropis ochrolongibracteata, also known as the long-bracted globeflower, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. The long-bracted globeflower has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and lower blood sugar levels, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Oxytropis ochrolongibracteata include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
